sprint.name: First Sale Sprint
sprint.goal: MILESTONE M1 REACHED 2026-07-13: checkout works, Stripe + PayPal connected in Payhip (T29 done). North Star: the FIRST SALE. Everything now points at getting the Starter Pack in front of buyers and making its first sale. Milestones: (M1) checkout works, connect a payment processor so Payhip can take money [T29]; (M2) Starter Pack launched, finish the build, upload, publish its buy section [T10, T26]; (M3) drive the sale, the online-presence push (platform posts) plus sales-funnel work become top priority once the Starter Pack is live [T30, T12, T14, T23]. The flagship course [T25] is ON HOLD until the Starter Pack records its first sales. PARALLEL REACH BUILD (Rafael, Divide & Conquer 2026-07-17): do NOT wait for M2 to build distribution. The reach engine is being built NOW, one channel at a time, easiest first, next starts only when the previous is fully running: (1) Bluesky [LIVE 2026-07-20], (2) Threads, (3) Instagram, (4) own blog hub; Pinterest handled in a parallel chat (Standard access GRANTED 2026-07-20). The live Survival Kit at /learn/ is already the destination these channels drive to; the Starter Pack joins as the flagship rung when it launches. One continuous board from the real project start (2026-07-08).

tasks.title: Landing page buy section
tasks.detail: Kit section on speak-like-yourself with Payhip buy button, dark brand

tasks.title: Starter Pack content build ($49.99)
tasks.detail: UMBRELLA, split 2026-07-15 on Rafael's call into three tangible legs: T32 Modules, T33 Support material, T34 Homework material. No work happens on this card; it closes when all three land, and it stays on the board because M2 plus T25/T26 gate on this id. Track B: the bridge from surviving a conversation to building your own sentences (L2, anchored on Eu). Ships as a two-part Payhip bundle (course + Companion), dark + light. Old blockers T20 (done) and T24 (syllabus locked in practice, all 18 modules built) retired at the split.

tasks.title: Real review quote on landing page
tasks.detail: Swap blockquote placeholder for an exact named Preply review

tasks.title: Landing analytics
tasks.detail: Track A3. GoatCounter (free, privacy-friendly) + per-pin src tags on index.html + learn/index.html so store visits, free-PDF opt-ins and Payhip button clicks become measurable.

tasks.title: Pinterest developer app
tasks.detail: App created (ID 1587877) on Business account. Privacy policy + URL verification blockers resolved 2026-07-10; app re-review passed.

tasks.title: Pinterest poster: Trial -> Standard upgrade
tasks.detail: DONE 2026-07-20: Standard access GRANTED for App 1587877 after two prior declines. The demo that won it: pinterest_demo.py rebuilt to do a REAL sandbox pin write + CDN read-back (the rejected demo only printed a JSON body and called nothing). Production POST /v5/pins now works, the 403 code 29 wall is gone. Ownership handed to the PARALLEL Pinterest chat (holds the OAuth token + app secret, auto-refresh working, sandbox artifacts retired); it runs pinterest_post.js as the LEADER (10 posts / 7 days via cadence.json) and writes pinterest-posted.json. Single-owner-per-platform: only that chat posts Pinterest. Poster is built and reconciled; final go on the daily schedule is the parallel chat's call.

tasks.title: Reddit/Quora question mining
tasks.detail: Weekly note of real questions matching corpus answers; queue-ready for Draft Factory

tasks.title: Opus orchestrator system
tasks.detail: orchestrate.ps1: bus reports -> Opus 4.8 decides -> dispatches codex/hermes/worker/scripts. 2x daily n8n cron + on-demand. Live 2026-07-09.

tasks.title: Deploy /learn/ store separation
tasks.detail: Track A1. git push web/ so the standalone store /learn/ goes live. Verify store renders, Payhip button works, zero Preply CTAs, assets 200, no mobile horizontal scroll. index.html stays teaching-only.

tasks.title: Email list: $0 opt-in + welcome email
tasks.detail: Track A2 (#1 compounding asset). Payhip $0 product for survival-phrases.pdf (captures email + delivers + unifies list). Swap free-PDF href + Starter Pack notify CTA in learn/index.html to the $0 URL. Welcome email introduces the ladder, soft-pitches the Kit.

tasks.title: Reusable production pipeline + content schema
tasks.detail: Track A5, highest-leverage parallel build. Generalize the Kit's one-off tooling into a parametrized pipeline driven by one content JSON: workbook generator (dark+light PDFs), edge-tts audio batch (3 speeds + category tracks, TTS only), gallery/thumbnail generator, reusable Payhip listing + buy-section HTML component. Deliverable: documented content-schema so syllabus output plugs straight in. Fable5 handoff owner.

tasks.title: Store polish: SEO, OG, favicon, a11y, mobile
tasks.detail: Track A4. learn/index.html: SEO title/meta, Open Graph + Twitter share image so pins/links preview well, favicon, accessibility pass, mobile QA. Make the store shareable and findable.

tasks.title: Bundle scaffolding + checkout cross-sell
tasks.detail: Track A6. Wire the bundle mechanism + Payhip cross-sell / order-bump so Kit buyers see the next rung. Plumbing ships now; the real bundle product waits on Starter Pack / flagship (T10/T25).

tasks.title: Demand repointing to /learn/
tasks.detail: Track A7. Repoint product-promoting content CTAs (Pinterest / Reddit / Shorts) to /learn/; keep the Preply teaching funnel on autopilot. Never steer Preply students off-platform; do steer the content audience to the products.

tasks.title: Approve Starter Pack + flagship syllabus
tasks.detail: The gate for Track B. Rafael is brainstorming the syllabus in a dedicated chat (kickoff prompt in Syllabus Brainstorm Prompt.md). Locks each module: title, one-line transformation, 3-6 subtopics, key phrases/structures, mapped to the 3-layer method (L1 Survival, L2 Eu self-expression, L3 fluency via pattern expansion). Arrives as content JSON per the T20 schema.

tasks.title: Flagship course content build ($97-197)
tasks.detail: ON HOLD until the Starter Pack (T10) records its FIRST SALES. Do not start building until money comes in from the Starter Pack; proving demand before investing the big build is the new direction. Track B. The full method end to end, L1 -> L2 -> L3: the product that replaces taking lessons with Rafael. Workbook PDFs + audio (TTS), optional short video/slides. Built through the T20 pipeline once un-held.

tasks.title: Publish Starter Pack buy section (launch)
tasks.detail: Milestone M2. Replace the store's Starter Pack coming-soon rung with its real buy section so the Pack is purchasable; this is the launch that opens the door to the first sale. Refocused to the Starter Pack only (unblocked from the flagship). Flagship buy section + the real bundle move to T25's follow-up once the flagship comes off hold.

tasks.title: First-sale push: platform posts + funnel
tasks.detail: Milestone M3, the engine for the first sale. Once the Starter Pack is live, make online presence + sales funnel the TOP priority: ramp platform posting (Pinterest, Reddit/Quora, YouTube Shorts) pointing at /learn/, tune the email welcome/nurture sequence to soft-pitch the Pack, and read analytics (T12) to turn traffic into the first Starter Pack sale. Iterate offer/copy until it converts.

tasks.title: Fix live Kit listing + covers wording
tasks.detail: Handoff open item. Product description FIXED by Rafael 2026-07-13 (no more human-voice claim). Remaining: re-render the PDF covers that still say 'spoken by a Brazilian' and re-upload the corrected PDFs to Payhip.

tasks.title: Resolve open product decisions
tasks.detail: RESOLVED by Rafael 2026-07-13. (1) Flagship format: Payhip PDF + audio bundle. (2) Email provider: Payhip. (3) Own domain: stay on GitHub Pages for now, move to a dedicated domain once sales start.

tasks.title: URGENT: connect Stripe or PayPal in Payhip
tasks.detail: DONE 2026-07-13: Rafael connected BOTH PayPal and Stripe to Payhip. Checkout is now unblocked account-wide, buy/download buttons render, Kit sales and email capture can happen. Milestone M1 (checkout works) reached. Was blocking everything since launch (0 orders explained).

tasks.title: Survival Kit two-voice audio revoice
tasks.detail: Replaced the single Antonio audio with a student voice choice: Donato (male) and Brenda (female). Full Azure TTS regen per voice: 127 phrases x 3 speeds + 11 category tracks + 7 game tracks + a ~12s intro (798 clips + 2 intros, 0 failures). README + both workbook PDFs refreshed with a pick-a-voice note, zip rebuilt (803 entries) and re-uploaded to Payhip; listing description updated for the male/female choice. Antonio pack archived, not shipped.

tasks.title: Starter Pack - Modules
tasks.detail: Main body of the course. 18 modules, L2 self-expression anchored on Eu, built from content/modules/m01-m18.json via assemble.py. Dark + light workbooks BUILT (142pp, 1018 phrases) but Rafael is reworking the look himself in the editable .docx, so the layout is not signed off. Module audio NOT built: the old 2349-clip Antonio run was parked and is gone from disk, so it needs a fresh run under the Marcelo + Fernanda standard. When it runs it MUST follow the preply-course-audio skill: Marcelo is normal-only (his slow/slower derive by ffmpeg atempo 0.75/0.60 from approved normals, never Azure), every approved take gets sha256-locked before anything else runs, and packaging ships an allowlist. Deriving cuts the API calls to ~1/3 and collapses the Marcelo QC asymmetry (approve one normal, the slow tiers inherit it).

tasks.title: Starter Pack - Support material
tasks.detail: Companion material. 1004-word Vocabulary Bank + full verb tables + 6 grammar flowcharts; Companion dark + light PDFs built (42pp). AUDIO COMPLETE 2026-07-16: 6024 clips (1004 words x 3 speeds x Fernanda + Marcelo) + 210 category tracks, Rafael flag-passed both voices (196 Marcelo words re-run and picked; 29 Fernanda words fixed via phoneme-corrected Brenda or revoiced to Thalita under her mask), folders masked to fernanda/marcelo. Ships inside build/Brazilian-Portuguese-Starter-Pack.zip (216.2 MB, 6241 entries, audited: 0 voice-id leaks, 0 rejected takes). WARNING before touching this again: 3099 clips are hand-approved and CANNOT be regenerated (Marcelo/Thalita re-roll on every request); they are sha256-locked and the builder refuses to overwrite them. Read the preply-course-audio skill first.

tasks.title: Starter Pack - Homework material
tasks.detail: Exercises per module. Not started, nothing on disk, never scoped until now. Needs a call from Rafael before it can be estimated properly: which exercise types per module, whether the answer key ships in the same PDF or separately, and whether homework rides inside the course workbook or becomes a third PDF alongside the course and the Companion.

tasks.title: Automated posting engine (cadence + approval pipeline)
tasks.detail: DONE 2026-07-20. The reusable machine that runs distribution without Rafael touching a platform. LEADER/FOLLOWER model (Rafael's call, not a shared clock): Pinterest LEADS at 10 posts / 7 days via cadence.json and never waits; other channels FOLLOW by mirror-by-id (post an item only once the leader has posted that same id from pinterest-posted.json), so a follower can never get ahead. Self-contained Node poster + Windows Scheduled Task per platform (replaced the n8n poster after its binary handoff errored in Rafael's build); this script+task pattern is the template for every next channel. APPROVAL PIPELINE: the Approval Queue is split by content-sharing platform group (Pinterest-Bsky-Threads-Instagram / Reddit-Quora / YouTube), each with Approval Queue/ (pending) + Approved/ (approved) subfolders; posting/approve_batch.js is the SINGLE writer of queue population, moving an approved batch to Approved/ and appending it to both platform queues (same ids -> mirror). Full contract in posting/CADENCE-SPEC.md.

tasks.title: Reach channel 1: Bluesky LIVE
tasks.detail: DONE 2026-07-20. First of the four reach channels (easiest first). Account @speaklikeyourself.bsky.social created; approved Batch-01 pin shipped and verified via the public API (image + alt + link facet + 2 tag facets by UTF-8 byte offset). Runs as the FOLLOWER in the leader/follower model: bluesky_post.js ignores cadence.json and mirrors Pinterest by id, fires 3x/day (10:00/14:00/18:00) to backfill then throttles to the leader's pace. Bots are allowed on Bluesky (self-labeled). This proved the script+Scheduled-Task template that channels 2-4 reuse.

tasks.title: Content batches + Monday cadence (2 weeks ahead)
tasks.detail: Ongoing. Rafael reviews batches every Monday and wants to stay a week ahead: TWO batches in hand each Monday (current + upcoming week) = a 20-unit runway target, 10-unit floor. The content engine was IDLE since 07-09; restarted 2026-07-20. The working pin pipeline (batch 02/03 proved it): author units.json (phrase|versus|quiz from the approved Survival Kit corpus, no theme repeats) -> scripts/render_pins.py -> queue-items.json -> on approval node approve_batch.js loads both queues. Batches 01, 02, 03 all authored + approved; both queues hold 30 mirrored items = runway 22 = TWO WEEKS AHEAD, buffer flag OK. Footer CTA locked across all three: 'Free Survival Phrases . Trial lessons: link in bio' (middle dot, no '+', so 'Free' does not read as covering the paid trial). OWNER of the Monday generate+flag job = HERMES (primary), Claude Code = BACKUP (batch_buffer_watch.js scheduled Mondays 09:30 flags if runway runs short or no Monday batch landed; it never auto-generates). OPEN GAP: draft_factory.py emits ONE templated concept per run, not 10 shippable units + captions, so it is not yet wired to this pipeline; batches 02/03 were hand-authored. Hermes needs a 10-unit generator to own Mondays cleanly.

tasks.title: Reach channel 2: Threads
tasks.detail: Next channel after Bluesky is fully running (build order: Bluesky -> Threads -> Instagram -> blog). Reuses the same pin cards (already named in the Pinterest-Bsky-Threads-Instagram content group) and the script+Scheduled-Task template from T35/T36. Meta app in dev mode gives Standard Access with NO app review for the own professional account; add it as a follower in the leader/follower model. Not started; starts when Bluesky is confirmed steady-state.

tasks.title: Reach channel 3: Instagram
tasks.detail: Channel 3 of 4. Same Meta app as Threads (dev-mode Standard Access, no app review for the own professional account, 100 posts/24h), same pin cards, same follower pattern. Backlog until Threads is running.

tasks.title: Reach channel 4: own blog hub
tasks.detail: Channel 4 of 4, the owned surface. Long-form (deliberately NOT in the shared pin group), approval-gated auto-publish. Governed by Google's scaled-content policy, so curated 1-2 pages/week only, not bulk. Backlog until Instagram is running.

tasks.title: Mission Control dashboard
tasks.detail: Requested by Rafael 2026-07-20: a single-page dark-mode one-stop-shop to run the whole post workflow without opening new chats. Platform status strip (LIVE/PLANNED + total posts per platform), latest-post-per-platform cards, batch approval section split current/upcoming week with skimmable PNG carousels + per-batch Approve button (POST -> existing approve_batch.js) + per-batch Flag textbox that writes a note into the batch folder, post-overview schedule (date + pin, due-timing from cadence.json), per-platform analytics panels. Handed to Fable 5 as an extensive build prompt, specced as a small local Node server + one HTML page (a static file cannot approve a batch or persist a flag), wired to the real queue/ledger/batch-folder JSON. Not built yet; awaiting Fable 5.
